mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during periods of declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized domestic demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and rising consumer prices indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in > 70% of observations during identified supply bottleneck periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS increases correlate with negative or flat 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 240 signal entrenched inflation that forces central bank tightening, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly consumption spending increases confirm robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and RSAFS momentum fails to predict positive 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during periods of stable interest rates reflects efficient capital allocation and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS remains stable, with mean forward returns below zero.
